the Ubiquitous order recently sent

ling the streets. And then the
mayor and board Of health talk Of
. dust nuisance and how to stop
u. How are the people to keep the
dust down In the outiayink sections
Of the city II not by sprinkIins with
the hose? A city water wagon or
street car sprinkling wagon is un-
known there.


Why is not the ordinance that
compels the street railway CON-
bines to sprinkle between tracks
enforced? The ordinance does not
confine the sprinkling to any por-
ton of the city, but covers all of
it alike, wherever street cars run.
Yet the ordinance is not enforced
as to the farther out residential
sections.


The other day woman residing
on Northwest boulevard telephoned
to the Washington Water Power
Co, asking it to sprinkle North
west boulevard between it's tracks.
Cant do it, lady; out of the ques-
tion,' came the response.


To the Editor of The Press:


Dear Sir: Did you know there
is more competition for the title
Lord Almighty" down at the city
hall? Yes, the secretary to Mr..
MIDGET claims it now.


The writer had an experience
with him over the telephone yees-
terday in which an endeavor was
made to learn where Mr.. Mudgett
might be found, and the secretary
ending the conversation with an
invition for me to come down and
fight him. The thing that riled
the secretary to the Johnsonizing
point was the remark that a pipe
line had been extended and water
Sprinkled in such a way at the
long bridge as to avoid the dust
nuisance at Mr.. Cowles place.
Evidently it hit him in sore
spot.


Is there no cure for employees be-
coming greater than Their REMIT
ance? Are the patrons of the water
service to be bluffed and bull
dozed? Because the people notice
